Class ExecutorMetrics

java.lang.Object
com.cmw.adaptive.metrics.ExecutorMetrics
public final class ExecutorMetrics extends Object
Collects runtime statistics and performance metrics for an AdaptiveExecutor.

Metrics include:

  • Task statistics
  • Worker statistics
  • Queue statistics
  • Scaling events
  • Execution timings
  • Throughput and utilization

All metrics are maintained using atomic structures and are safe for concurrent access.

    Description

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    int
    activeWorkers()
    Returns the number of currently active (busy) workers.
    double
    averageExecutionTimeMillis()
    Returns the average execution time of completed tasks.
    double
    averageQueueWaitMillis()
    Returns the average time tasks have spent waiting in the queue.
    long
    completedTasks()
    Returns the total number of tasks that have completed successfully.
    int
    currentQueueSize()
    Returns the current number of tasks waiting in the queue.
    int
    currentWorkers()
    Returns the current total number of workers (active + idle).
    long
    failedTasks()
    Returns the total number of tasks that have failed during execution.
    void
    incrementCompleted()
    Increments the count of completed tasks by one.
    void
    incrementFailed()
    Increments the count of failed tasks by one.
    void
    incrementRejected()
    Increments the count of rejected tasks by one.
    void
    incrementSubmitted()
    Increments the count of submitted tasks by one.
    double
    longestExecutionTimeMillis()
    Returns the longest execution time among completed tasks.
    double
    longestQueueWaitMillis()
    Returns the longest time any task has spent waiting in the queue.
    int
    peakQueueSize()
    Returns the maximum number of tasks that have been waiting in the queue simultaneously.
    int
    peakWorkers()
    Returns the peak number of workers that have been active simultaneously.
    void
    recordExecutionTime(long executionTimeNanos)
    Should be called when a task completes execution to record its execution time.
    void
    recordQueueWaitTime(long waitTimeNanos)
    Should be called when a task is dequeued for execution to record its wait time in the queue.
    long
    rejectedTasks()
    Returns the total number of tasks that were rejected due to capacity limits.
    void
    scaleDown()
    Should be called when the executor scales down by removing a worker.
    long
    scaleDownEvents()
    Returns the total number of scale-down events that have occurred since startup.
    void
    scaleUp()
    Should be called when the executor scales up by adding a new worker.
    long
    scaleUpEvents()
    Returns the total number of scale-up events that have occurred since startup.
    long
    submittedTasks()
    Returns the total number of tasks that have been submitted for execution.
    double
    successRate()
    Returns the percentage of successfully completed tasks.
    double
    throughputTasksPerSecond()
    Returns the average throughput since executor startup.
    double
    totalExecutionTimeMillis()
    Returns the total execution time of all completed tasks in milliseconds.
    double
    totalQueueWaitTimeMillis()
    Returns the total time tasks have spent waiting in the queue in milliseconds.
    void
    updateQueueSize(int queueSize)
    Updates the current queue size and checks if it exceeds the previous peak.
    long
    uptimeSeconds()
    Returns the uptime of the executor in seconds.
    void
    workerBusy()
    Should be called when a worker thread starts executing a task.
    void
    workerIdle()
    Should be called when a worker thread finishes executing a task and becomes idle.
    long
    workersCreated()
    Returns the total number of worker threads that have been created since startup.
    long
    workersDestroyed()
    Returns the total number of worker threads that have been destroyed since startup.
    void
    workerStarted()
    Should be called when a worker thread starts executing a task.
    void
    workerStopped()
    Should be called when a worker thread finishes executing a task and becomes idle.
    double
    workerUtilizationPercent()
    Estimates worker utilization over the executor lifetime.

    • ExecutorMetrics

      public ExecutorMetrics()

  • Method Details

    • submittedTasks

      public long submittedTasks()
      Returns the total number of tasks that have been submitted for execution.
      Returns:
      total submitted tasks

    • completedTasks

      public long completedTasks()
      Returns the total number of tasks that have completed successfully.
      Returns:
      total completed tasks

    • failedTasks

      public long failedTasks()
      Returns the total number of tasks that have failed during execution.
      Returns:
      total failed tasks

    • rejectedTasks

      public long rejectedTasks()
      Returns the total number of tasks that were rejected due to capacity limits.
      Returns:
      total rejected tasks

    • activeWorkers

      public int activeWorkers()
      Returns the number of currently active (busy) workers.
      Returns:
      active worker count

    • currentWorkers

      public int currentWorkers()
      Returns the current total number of workers (active + idle).
      Returns:
      current worker count

    • peakWorkers

      public int peakWorkers()
      Returns the peak number of workers that have been active simultaneously.
      Returns:
      peak worker count

    • workersCreated

      public long workersCreated()
      Returns the total number of worker threads that have been created since startup.
      Returns:
      total workers created

    • workersDestroyed

      public long workersDestroyed()
      Returns the total number of worker threads that have been destroyed since startup.
      Returns:
      total workers destroyed

    • currentQueueSize

      public int currentQueueSize()
      Returns the current number of tasks waiting in the queue.
      Returns:
      current queue size

    • peakQueueSize

      public int peakQueueSize()
      Returns the maximum number of tasks that have been waiting in the queue simultaneously.
      Returns:
      peak queue size

    • scaleUpEvents

      public long scaleUpEvents()
      Returns the total number of scale-up events that have occurred since startup.
      Returns:
      total scale-up events

    • scaleDownEvents

      public long scaleDownEvents()
      Returns the total number of scale-down events that have occurred since startup.
      Returns:
      total scale-down events

    • totalExecutionTimeMillis

      public double totalExecutionTimeMillis()
      Returns the total execution time of all completed tasks in milliseconds.
      Returns:
      total execution time in milliseconds

    • totalQueueWaitTimeMillis

      public double totalQueueWaitTimeMillis()
      Returns the total time tasks have spent waiting in the queue in milliseconds.
      Returns:
      total queue wait time in milliseconds

    • averageExecutionTimeMillis

      public double averageExecutionTimeMillis()
      Returns the average execution time of completed tasks.
      Returns:
      average execution time in milliseconds

    • longestExecutionTimeMillis

      public double longestExecutionTimeMillis()
      Returns the longest execution time among completed tasks.
      Returns:
      longest execution time in milliseconds

    • averageQueueWaitMillis

      public double averageQueueWaitMillis()
      Returns the average time tasks have spent waiting in the queue.
      Returns:
      average queue wait time in milliseconds

    • longestQueueWaitMillis

      public double longestQueueWaitMillis()
      Returns the longest time any task has spent waiting in the queue.
      Returns:
      longest queue wait time in milliseconds

    • throughputTasksPerSecond

      public double throughputTasksPerSecond()
      Returns the average throughput since executor startup.
      Returns:
      completed tasks per second

    • successRate

      public double successRate()
      Returns the percentage of successfully completed tasks.
      Returns:
      success rate percentage

    • uptimeSeconds

      public long uptimeSeconds()
      Returns the uptime of the executor in seconds.
      Returns:
      uptime in seconds

    • workerUtilizationPercent

      public double workerUtilizationPercent()
      Estimates worker utilization over the executor lifetime.
      Returns:
      utilization percentage

    • incrementSubmitted

      public void incrementSubmitted()
      Increments the count of submitted tasks by one.

    • incrementCompleted

      public void incrementCompleted()
      Increments the count of completed tasks by one.

    • incrementFailed

      public void incrementFailed()
      Increments the count of failed tasks by one.

    • incrementRejected

      public void incrementRejected()
      Increments the count of rejected tasks by one.

    • workerStarted

      public void workerStarted()
      Should be called when a worker thread starts executing a task. Increments active worker count and updates peak if necessary.

    • workerStopped

      public void workerStopped()
      Should be called when a worker thread finishes executing a task and becomes idle. Decrements active worker count and current worker count.

    • workerBusy

      public void workerBusy()
      Should be called when a worker thread starts executing a task. Increments active worker count.

    • workerIdle

      public void workerIdle()
      Should be called when a worker thread finishes executing a task and becomes idle. Decrements active worker count.

    • scaleUp

      public void scaleUp()
      Should be called when the executor scales up by adding a new worker. Increments the scale-up event count.

    • scaleDown

      public void scaleDown()
      Should be called when the executor scales down by removing a worker. Increments the scale-down event count.

    • updateQueueSize

      public void updateQueueSize(int queueSize)
      Updates the current queue size and checks if it exceeds the previous peak.
      Parameters:
      queueSize - current number of tasks in the queue

    • recordExecutionTime

      public void recordExecutionTime(long executionTimeNanos)
      Should be called when a task completes execution to record its execution time.
      Parameters:
      executionTimeNanos - execution time of the task in nanoseconds

    • recordQueueWaitTime

      public void recordQueueWaitTime(long waitTimeNanos)
      Should be called when a task is dequeued for execution to record its wait time in the queue.
      Parameters:
      waitTimeNanos - time the task spent waiting in the queue in nanoseconds
